title: include buildkit support to target and skip specific stages inside multi-stage build
body:
According to your development plan you are aiming for "feature parity with other OCI image and container build systems". Currently we are using docker to perform our builds, but we would like to switch to buildah, since Red Hat doesn't officially support Docker anymore. Unfortunately currently `buildad bud` doesn't seem to be compatible with [buildkit](https://github.com/moby/buildkit), which allows us to build [specific stages of a multi-stage build (not just all layers until `target`, but only the ones needed) with docker](https://github.com/docker/cli/issues/1134#issuecomment-406449342). Any plan to introduce buildkit support?

Thanks a lot for your effort!

comments.body: Do we have a pointer to a Dockerfile and build context where we can reproduce this?  We don't currently evaluate stages and skip ones that don't matter to the target stage, but one that I'd contrive would necessarily be pretty simple.

Example of such a Dockerfile:

```dockerfile
FROM scratch AS file1
ADD file1.txt /file.txt

FROM scratch AS file2
ADD file2.txt /file.txt

FROM ubuntu AS alt1
COPY --from=file1 / /

FROM ubuntu AS alt2
COPY --from=file2 / /
```

Building image targering `alt2` in the example results in building only `file2` and `alt2` in buildkit and all four of them using Buildah.

This is a major blocker for me when looking into switching from Docker to Buildah.
Would be helpful if someone in community opened a PR, or if we can free up some time for @nalind to work on this.

Looks like a duplicate / shorter and later version of https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2469 to me.

> Looks like a duplicate / shorter and later version of https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2469 to me.

It is yes, you can close my request then. Any plans to implement 2469 soon?
